Description: Description
Worknote: Worknote
Resolution Note: Steps

Description: Store checkout calls to api.storeorderpro.internal failing; name resolution errors observed in app and network logs.
Worknote: Worknote: Ran nslookup on api.storeorderpro.internal from app node and jump host; both timed out. Verified VPC DNS forwarders healthy; CoreDNS pod in cluster showed high restarts. Flushed DNS cache on app nodes; created temporary hosts override for critical nodes and restored service while DNS was fixed upstream.
Resolution Note: 1) Validated host reachability with ping and curl to confirm name resolution vs. network path. 2) Checked nslookup/dig for api.storeorderpro.internal from app nodes and bastion. 3) Inspected cluster DNS (CoreDNS) pod status/restarts and logs. 4) Flushed DNS cache (systemd-resolved/nscd) on app nodes. 5) Applied temporary /etc/hosts mapping for api.storeorderpro.internal to current VIP. 6) Rolled CoreDNS deployment and confirmed healthy endpoints. 7) Removed hosts override after DNS propagation and verified application health.

Description: Payment gateway calls to payment-gateway.internal failing intermittently; DNS queries returning SERVFAIL from upstream resolver.
Worknote: Worknote: Compared resolver paths (node-resolver → CoreDNS → VPC resolver). Found stale upstream zone delegation. Coordinated with platform team to correct internal zone NS records. Implemented short-term conditional forwarder as a mitigation; monitored to stable.
Resolution Note: 1) Reproduced failure with dig payment-gateway.internal +trace to identify failing hop. 2) Verified CoreDNS configmaps for stub/forward rules. 3) Checked internal zone delegation and NS records in the private DNS. 4) Added conditional forwarder to healthy resolver set. 5) Cleared node caches and restarted pods to refresh DNS entries. 6) Validated end-to-end transactions post-fix.

Description: User authentication spikes failing as auth.service.internal could not be resolved in certain subnets.
Worknote: Worknote: Identified subnet-specific resolver using legacy DHCP options pointing to retired DNS. Updated DHCP options set to standardized resolvers and forced lease renew on affected nodes. Confirmed consistent resolution across subnets.
Resolution Note: 1) Tested nslookup/dig for auth.service.internal from multiple subnets. 2) Compared resolv.conf and DHCP-provided DNS servers across nodes. 3) Updated DHCP options to approved resolver list and applied. 4) Renewed leases (dhclient) and flushed caches. 5) Validated resolution and sign-in flows across AZs. 6) Opened change record to track DHCP standardization.

Description: End?user reports AVD session dropping every 5 7 minutes; degradation noticed in screen rendering and input response. Diagnostics indicate increased RTT and packet loss consistent with user?side network instability.
Worknote: Worknote: 1) Collected AVD diagnostics from Azure Monitor (Connections, Network Data Logs, Graphics Performance). 2) Verified host registration and AVD agent health no errors found. 3) Ran Log Analytics queries identified recurring network packet loss on client side. 4) Guided user to switch from home Wi?Fi to mobile hotspot for validation.
Resolution Note: 1) Confirmed root cause as poor client?side Wi?Fi signal and packet loss. 2) User switched to alternate network (5G hotspot) reducing RTT. 3) Session monitored for 30 minutes no further drops observed. 4) Shared network best?practice guidelines. 5) Marked incident as resolved after stable verification.
